brief_justification: Paper A trains neural visual world models for model-based RL and adds an auxiliary pixel-reconstruction loss on segmented concept regions to improve dynamical fidelity, whereas paper B recovers a stochastic discount factor (pricing kernel) from S&P 500 option prices and rationalizes its W-shape via stochastic-volatility asset-pricing theory; the only overlap is the generic notion of 'a model that predicts,' with no shared governing equation, estimation principle, or role-for-role mechanism, so no coherent mapping holds.
